concerns.text: Phase 6 introduces hard-coded chemical constants (bond lengths, radii, electronegativity) and augments the PolymerGraph schema with these features. This directly violates FR-001 and Constitution Principle VI, which strictly mandate that node/edge features be limited to 'atom type, hybridization, bond type' derived from 2D SMILES. The tasks silently relax the feature constraint defined in the spec without a formal scope change.

Task T052 implements a 'Physics-Informed Loss' term penalizing deviations from 'free_volume_fraction'. This introduces a new loss function and physical constraint not present in FR-003 (which specifies a standard message-passing GNN) or any other functional requirement. This is a semantic weakening of the model definition and an unapproved scope expansion.

Task T009a defines the 'PolyPerme Physics Simulation Generator' as a primary data source. While the spec allows a fallback, the plan and tasks treat this simulation as the primary validation path, effectively replacing the FR-001 requirement to 'load polymer data from NIST and PubChem' with synthetic data generation. This violates the 'Verified Accuracy' principle by substituting experimental ground truth with simulated values without explicit spec amendment.

Task T040 requires hard-coding chemical constants from the 'CRC Handbook' into code. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene), which states 'No data may be modified in place' and requires external data to be checksummed and versioned. Hard-coding constants bypasses the required derivation tracking and checksumming for external data sources.
